title: How we review traditional medicine research before publication
excerpt: Our internal process for selecting studies, verifying context, and presenting findings responsibly.
author: PhotoMed Editorial
content: PhotoMed reviews research with one goal: make knowledge useful without overstating certainty. We prioritize peer-reviewed publications and institutional reports, then cross-check claims against independent sources.

Every article is written in plain language first, then reviewed for scientific precision. When evidence is limited, we state that directly. We avoid absolute wording and avoid making clinical promises.
Our approach is to preserve and contextualize documented knowledge. We do not publish treatment instructions as medical advice, and we always include safety framing where appropriate.

title: Preserving medicinal plant knowledge across generations
excerpt: Why documentation quality is as important as documentation quantity.
author: PhotoMed Team
content: Traditional plant knowledge is often transmitted orally. As communities urbanize and elders pass on, critical context can disappear quickly if it is not documented carefully.
We focus on preserving not just names of plants, but context: local usage, preparation practices, known cautions, and regional relevance. Context is what makes information usable.
Digitization should support communities, not replace them. The goal is continuity: helping knowledge remain available, legible, and useful to future generations.

title: Designing trust in health knowledge products
excerpt: Product choices that make health information clearer and more responsible.
author: Product at PhotoMed
content: Trust starts with clarity. We keep interfaces simple, label confidence, and avoid overstating certainty. Users should understand what the system knows and where caution is needed.
Responsible design also means reducing friction in critical moments: straightforward flows, readable copy, and immediate access to important context.
Our product philosophy is practical: useful information, clear limits, and a respectful bridge between documented traditional knowledge and modern digital access.
